goal: Inline the active subviews directly below the active primary sidebar item instead of rendering them in a separate workspace block.
deliverable: Inline expanded second-level nav beneath the active first-level item
Removal of the separate submenu area
Preservation of existing badges and default-landing behavior
Verification that the current baseline stays green
constraints: Preserve current hash and remembered-subview behavior
Limit the round to shell presentation changes
acceptance_criteria: The active first-level item expands inline with its second-level entries
The separate second-level sidebar section is removed
Badges still render on meaningful subviews
product_acceptance_criteria: The sidebar reads like one coherent navigation tree
Users can move through first-level and second-level items without visually switching navigation zones
manual_spot_checks: Switch to operations and confirm its subviews appear directly under the operations item
Switch to reports and confirm its subviews appear directly under the reports item
Confirm badges still appear on tracker/procurement/exceptions when counts are present
Confirm explicit hashes still open the requested subview
completion_language_policy: Only claim completion after the shell change is in place and validation reruns successfully.
delivery_truth_contract: Describe only the inline submenu presentation change and the verification that actually ran.
inferred_assumptions: The user prefers a single-tree sidebar mental model over a separate submenu card.
The prior badge and default-landing work should remain, only the presentation should change.
